articles.title: Trump Faces Rare G.O.P. Revolt, and Democrats Mock Their Own Party’s Report
articles.summary: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

articles.title: Palestinians drop bid for a senior U.N. role after U.S. pressure
articles.summary: The Palestinian delegation to the United Nations has dropped its bid to secure a top U.N. job after the U.S. threatens to revoke their visas.

articles.title: GOP races to fund immigration enforcement. And, U.S. indicts former Cuban president
articles.summary: Republicans are racing to pass a $72 billion immigration enforcement package. And, the U.S. has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two planes.

articles.title: Oil Prices Jump on Impasse Over Reopening the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Nearly three months since the fighting began, disagreements remain over the fate of Iran’s uranium stockpile and transit fees for the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Warsh to Take Charge of Fed Facing Rising Inflation Threat
articles.summary: The economic backdrop that Kevin M. Warsh inherits as chair of the Federal Reserve does not call for the interest rate cuts that President Trump wants.

articles.title: What is the 'tarps off' trend that men are engaging in? Find out in the quiz
articles.summary: Plus: The L.A. mayoral race, AI court battles, extinct animals and Eurovision!

articles.title: 'We'll never know if he could have been saved.' The gaps in Trump's rural health fund
articles.summary: Republicans promise that $50 billion in new health funding will help rural America. But it's not expected to aid the years-long effort in North Carolina's Martin County to reopen its only hospital.

articles.title: As voters prioritize cost of living, focus on abortion evolves in midterm elections
articles.summary: There's been a shift in attention in the party ahead of the midterms as voters rank affordability the top issue. That raises questions about what an evolving message on reproductive rights looks like.

articles.title: A stranger's compliment helped her feel confident after chemo
articles.summary: When Pat Gentile began to grow out her hair after chemotherapy, she was nervous to go to work for the first time without a wig. An unexpected encounter with a convenience store stranger changed that.

articles.title: Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' ends with a swan song and a giant wormhole
articles.summary: With a dash of sci-fi, Colbert ended his CBS late-night show with a powerful musical sendoff featuring Paul McCartney, Elvis Costello and Jon Batiste.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: Senate breaks for recess, delaying vote on ICE funding, DNC releases 'autopsy' of 2024 presidential election, a look at Trump's political -- and personal -- interest in Cuba.

articles.title: Italy's prime minister wants Israel to apologize for treatment of flotilla activists
articles.summary: Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni wants an apology from Israel over the treatment of Italian citizens on a flotilla bound for Gaza.

articles.title: 'That's Aaron's redbud': Father remembers son with tree planted in his honor
articles.summary: A father talks about the tree in his front yard that honors the memory of his son who was killed in Afghanistan.

articles.title: UN gravely concerned by an Afghan Taliban law that has provisions on child marriage
articles.summary: The United Nations expressed "grave concern" on Thursday about a new law issued by Afghanistan's Taliban government on separation in marriage which includes provisions on child marriage.

articles.title: Trump says he's sending 5,000 more troops to Poland
articles.summary: President Trump's announcement stirred confusion in Europe following weeks of changing statements from his administration about reducing the American military footprint in Europe.

articles.title: Rubio doubtful of diplomacy with Cuba as Trump raises new threat of military action
articles.summary: President Donald Trump and America's top diplomat on Thursday again raised the specter of U.S. military intervention in Cuba, a day after the administration announced criminal charges against the isla

articles.title: Iran and Oman in Talks Over Strait of Hormuz Ship Payment System
articles.summary: The discussions suggest that the United States and the Iranian government may not be close to reaching a deal to end a war that has badly damaged the global economy.

articles.title: Audit Immunity for Trump Family Puts I.R.S. in a Bind
articles.summary: Federal law prohibits the Internal Revenue Service from halting an audit at the direction of the president or his aides.

articles.title: In a Rarity, Republicans Stand Up to Trump
articles.summary: President Trump faced a wall of opposition from Senate G.O.P. lawmakers, in part over his plan to create a $1.8 billion fund to reward his allies.

articles.title: GOP Pulls Measure on Trump’s War Powers in Iran, Lacking Votes to Defeat It
articles.summary: House Republican leaders abruptly scrapped a planned vote on a measure to direct President Trump to end the conflict or win authorization for it, amid party defections and absences.

articles.title: Inside the Senate G.O.P. Meltdown Over Trump’s Fund
articles.summary: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, went to Capitol Hill to allay Republicans’ concerns over a fund to pay people who claim government mistreatment. It did not go well.

articles.title: Trump's Raúl Castro indictment sends a signal to Cuba
articles.summary: The indictment of Raul Castro is the latest move by the Trump administration to ratchet up pressure on the Cuban government.

articles.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
articles.summary: Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
